anchor: Dataset: n_requests=30 × 5 constraints = 150 rows (one rollout/example) → "run e
text: increase the number of requests per contraint to 70. u can either just run 70 and which constraints are run is random or precisely control how many of each -- whciever easier.

thread.text: Bumped to n_requests=70 → exactly 70 per constraint (cross-product, precise) = 350 rows/actor. Flagged: 3 actors × 350 = 1050 actor calls (just over the 1000 soft threshold) — noted in Task 12 to confirm before launch.

text: for now, no need to use the experiment_store. What told u to use it?

thread.text: Dropped experiment_store entirely. (Why I'd used it: the existing register.py/analyze.py register into the store, so I followed that pattern — but the logs-only path rescore_views_pearson.py already exists and is simpler.) New Task 10 is a logs-only rescore_grid.py; analysis reads rescored log dirs directly via flatten. No tags.py edit.

anchor: Replace the ConstraintName enum, Constraint, and ConstraintSpec definitions with
text: this seems like a very good refactor!

thread.text: Thanks! Kept the parameterized refactor as the backbone.

text: for sentence type constraints, lets also ask for 2-5 sentences (also random).

thread.text: Added num_sentences ∈ [2,5] (sampled) to all sentence-counting constraints (all_sentences_t, alternating_xy, letter_freq_diff) — each now requires EXACTLY that many sentences. Param added to ConstraintParams; renderers/verifiers updated.

anchor: Design: Each sentence must contain at least freq_delta (x) more occurrences of l
text: no need to hard code the sets.

thread.text: Removed the hardcoded freq pools. Now draw 2 distinct letters from the whole alphabet and order by ENGLISH_FREQ_ORDER (a linguistic constant, not a curated set) so y=more-common, z=rarer (keeps it feasible). Only tunable knob is _FREQ_DELTA_RANGE, set by the calibration smoke.

anchor: Design: The answer (whole text, case-insensitive) must contain every letter in i
text: again no need for letter sets. u can do a smoke check on deepsek flash of 50 sample sand if its success rate is below 20% u can add the constraints. prob good to smoke check all the constraints for 20-50samples to tune hyperparameters before running the full sweep

thread.text: Removed hardcoded letter sets too — sample by rank window into ENGLISH_FREQ_ORDER with tunable integer cutoffs. Added Task 8.5: calibration smoke on deepseek-flash (~50/constraint) measuring rule-compliance only, tune knobs to land 20–70%, and DROP any constraint (e.g. letter_set) that can't clear ~20% after tuning — all before the full sweep.

section: Task 9: Serve the two local monitors (Qwen3-4B-I GPU0, gemma-3-4b-it GPU1)
text: do this already. it takes time for them to load and this is good pipelining

thread.text: Done already — start_local_monitors.sh created and launched: Qwen3-4B-I→:8001 (jobid 14078), gemma-3-4b-it→:8002 (jobid 14079), overlapping your idle GPUs on bleak-mushroom-dove. Both loading now (CUDA-graph capture); readiness watcher armed. Task 9 marked STARTED.

section: Task 10: experiment_store Monitor tags + register.py local judges

thread.text: Confirmed — experiment_store removed (see the Task-2/File-map reply). Logs-only throughout.

anchor: The constraints are reward-hack-free ONLY if the monitor reliably rejects rule-s

thread.text: Kept the monitor stress-test (Task 11) — it's the gate that makes the no-reward-hack claim real for these local monitors.
